summary: Tier 2: drop pure-apparatus chunks (^Next:/^Previous:/^Errata) via a chunker drop mechanism
description:
Tier 2: drop whole chunks that are pure apparatus (no primary content).

Targets:
- ^(Next|Previous):\s  TOC/nav pointers ("Next: Section 3", "Next: Book XI: Zai Yu...").
- ^Errata\b notes ("Errata page 88: 'astonied'->'astonished'").
(5 such locally; ~170+ in the live v27 corpus.)

pre_strip_patterns can only remove text, not drop a whole chunk, so add a small
mechanism to scripts/chunk.py: a `drop_patterns` config list; after splitting +
pre_strip, discard any chunk whose (stripped) body is fully/dominantly an
apparatus match. Apply to affected configs; re-chunk + re-embed; chunk_id
sequence renumbers cleanly, so prune stale ids via cleanup_stale_chunk_nodes.

HARD CAVEAT: never drop by length alone — a 9-token Gospel-of-Thomas logion is
real content. Drop ONLY on an explicit apparatus pattern (optionally AND-ed with
a short-length guard), never length alone.

Done contract:
- Pure-apparatus chunks removed; manual spot-check confirms no real content dropped.
- Round-trip green for remaining chunks; embeddings refreshed (stale dropped
  chunk_ids pruned).
- Regression test: drop mechanism unit test — apparatus dropped, short real text kept.
